payload.active_prompt: ok what is the stage play panel?
payload.selected_final_answer: The Stage Play Panel is a user interface component that facilitates interaction with the Stage Play Badge Graph, which reflects real-time evidence from the live world into a transient format. This panel allows users to project the current live interpretation of the Stage Play, utilizing capabilities such as reflecting the active badge graph and managing checkpoint requests. It serves as a bridge between the user and the underlying data, enabling users to engage with the Stage Play's dynamic elements and make informed decisions based on the live data presented. The responsibilities of the Stage Play Panel include managing the display of the badge graph, handling user inputs for checkpoint requests, and providing a visual representation of the current state of the Stage Play. It operates within a broader workflow that involves querying live sources and interpreting the results in a way that is accessible to users. The evidence establishes that the Stage Play Panel is integral to the functionality of the Stage Play system, acting as both a UI surface and a tool for runtime interactions. It is designed to support various actions related to the Stage Play, such as routing narratives and utilizing live interpretations, while also adhering to defined test contracts to ensure reliability and accuracy in its operations (docs/stage-play-badge-graph.md; client/src/components/panels/StagePlayBadgeGraphPanel.tsx).

current_turn_events.text: The Stage Play Panel is a user interface component that facilitates interaction with the Stage Play Badge Graph, which reflects real-time evidence from the live world into a transient format. This panel allows users to project the current live interpretation of the Stage Play, utilizing capabilities such as reflecting the active badge graph and managing checkpoint requests. It serves as a bridge between the user and the underlying data, enabling users to engage with the Stage Play's dynamic elements and make informed decisions based on the live data presented. The responsibilities of the Stage Play Panel include managing the display of the badge graph, handling user inputs for checkpoint requests, and providing a visual representation of the current state of the Stage Play. It operates within a broader workflow that involves querying live sources and interpreting the results in a way that is accessible to users. The evidence establishes that the Stage Play Panel is integral to the functionality of the Stage Play system, acting as both a UI surface and a tool for runtime interactions. It is designed to support various actions related to the Stage Play, such as routing narratives and utilizing live interpretations, while also adhering to defined test contracts to ensure reliability and accuracy in its operations (docs/stage-play-badge-graph.md; client/src/components/panels/StagePlayBadgeGraphPanel.tsx).

surfaces.non_stream_text: The Stage Play Panel is a user interface component that facilitates interaction with the Stage Play Badge Graph, which reflects real-time evidence from the live world into a transient format. This panel allows users to project the current live interpretation of the Stage Play, utilizing capabilities such as reflecting the active badge graph and managing checkpoint requests. It serves as a bridge between the user and the underlying data, enabling users to engage with the Stage Play's dynamic elements and make informed decisions based on the live data presented. The responsibilities of the Stage Play Panel include managing the display of the badge graph, handling user inputs for checkpoint requests, and providing a visual representation of the current state of the Stage Play. It operates within a broader workflow that involves querying live sources and interpreting the results in a way that is accessible to users. The evidence establishes that the Stage Play Panel is integral to the functionality of the Stage Play system, acting as both a UI surface and a tool for runtime interactions. It is designed to support various actions related to the Stage Play, such as routing narratives and utilizing live interpretations, while also adhering to defined test contracts to ensure reliability and accuracy in its operations (docs/stage-play-badge-graph.md; client/src/components/panels/StagePlayBadgeGraphPanel.tsx).

repo_code_evidence_answer.answer_text: The Stage Play Panel is a user interface component that facilitates interaction with the Stage Play Badge Graph, which reflects real-time evidence from the live world into a transient format. This panel allows users to project the current live interpretation of the Stage Play, utilizing capabilities such as reflecting the active badge graph and managing checkpoint requests. It serves as a bridge between the user and the underlying data, enabling users to engage with the Stage Play's dynamic elements and make informed decisions based on the live data presented. The responsibilities of the Stage Play Panel include managing the display of the badge graph, handling user inputs for checkpoint requests, and providing a visual representation of the current state of the Stage Play. It operates within a broader workflow that involves querying live sources and interpreting the results in a way that is accessible to users. The evidence establishes that the Stage Play Panel is integral to the functionality of the Stage Play system, acting as both a UI surface and a tool for runtime interactions. It is designed to support various actions related to the Stage Play, such as routing narratives and utilizing live interpretations, while also adhering to defined test contracts to ensure reliability and accuracy in its operations (docs/stage-play-badge-graph.md; client/src/components/panels/StagePlayBadgeGraphPanel.tsx).
